groups.name: APT28
groups.aliases: Fancy Bear, Sofacy, Sednit, STRONTIUM, Pawn Storm
groups.description: APT28 is a threat group attributed to Russia's General Staff Main Intelligence Directorate (GRU) 85th Main Special Service Center (GTsSS) military unit 26165. This group has been active since at least 2004.

groups.name: APT29
groups.aliases: Cozy Bear, The Dukes, NOBELIUM, Midnight Blizzard
groups.description: APT29 is a threat group attributed to Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR). They have operated since at least 2008, often targeting government networks in Europe and NATO member countries.

groups.name: Lazarus Group
groups.aliases: HIDDEN COBRA, Guardians of Peace, ZINC, Diamond Sleet
groups.description: Lazarus Group is a North Korean state-sponsored cyber threat group attributed to the Reconnaissance General Bureau. The group has been active since at least 2009 and has conducted operations for espionage, financial theft, and sabotage.

groups.name: FIN7
groups.aliases: Carbanak Group, Navigator Group, Carbon Spider
groups.description: FIN7 is a financially-motivated threat group that has been active since at least 2013. FIN7 has primarily targeted the U.S. retail, restaurant, and hospitality sectors.

groups.name: Carbanak
groups.description: Carbanak is a cybercriminal group that has used the Carbanak backdoor and is known for targeting financial institutions. The group has stolen over $1 billion from banks worldwide.

groups.name: Turla
groups.aliases: Waterbug, Venomous Bear, KRYPTON, Secret Blizzard
groups.description: Turla is a Russian-based threat group that has been active since at least 2004 and is attributed to Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB). Turla is known for targeting government, military, and research organizations.

groups.name: Sandworm Team
groups.aliases: ELECTRUM, Telebots, IRIDIUM, VOODOO BEAR, Seashell Blizzard
groups.description: Sandworm Team is a destructive threat group attributed to Russia's GRU Main Center for Special Technologies (GTsST). The group has conducted attacks on critical infrastructure including the 2015 and 2016 Ukraine power grid attacks.

groups.name: APT32
groups.description: APT32 is a Vietnamese threat group that has been active since at least 2014. The group has targeted private sector industries as well as government, political, and journalistic entities in Southeast Asia.

groups.name: Dragonfly 2.0
groups.description: Dragonfly 2.0 is a threat group that has targeted energy sector and industrial control systems in North America and Europe since at least 2015.

groups.name: Kimsuky
groups.aliases: Velvet Chollima, Black Banshee, Emerald Sleet
groups.description: Kimsuky is a North Korean threat group that has been active since at least 2012. The group focuses on intelligence gathering and has targeted South Korean government entities, think tanks, and individuals.

groups.name: menuPass
groups.aliases: APT10, Stone Panda, Red Apollo, POTASSIUM
groups.description: menuPass is a threat group attributed to the Chinese Ministry of State Security's (MSS) Tianjin State Security Bureau. The group has been active since at least 2006 and is known for targeting managed service providers.

groups.name: APT41
groups.aliases: Barium, Winnti Group, Wicked Panda, Brass Typhoon
groups.description: APT41 is a Chinese state-sponsored espionage group that also conducts financially motivated operations. APT41 has been active since at least 2012 and has targeted healthcare, telecom, technology, and gaming sectors.

groups.name: Earth Lusca
groups.description: Earth Lusca is a suspected China-based cyber espionage group that has been active since at least 2021. The group targets government institutions, media, and academic organizations.

groups.name: APT38
groups.description: APT38 is a North Korean state-sponsored threat group that specializes in financial cyber operations. Since at least 2014, the group has targeted banks, financial institutions, casinos, cryptocurrency exchanges, and SWIFT system endpoints.

groups.name: Leviathan
groups.aliases: APT40, TEMP.Periscope, TEMP.Jumper, Kryptonite Panda, Gingham Typhoon
groups.description: Leviathan is a Chinese state-sponsored cyber espionage group attributed to the Ministry of State Security's (MSS) Hainan State Security Department. The group has targeted maritime industries, defense, and government entities.

groups.name: Threat Group-3390
groups.aliases: TG-3390, Emissary Panda, APT27, Iron Tiger
groups.description: Threat Group-3390 is a Chinese threat group that has targeted organizations in aerospace, government, defense, technology, energy, and manufacturing sectors. The group has been active since at least 2010.

software.name: Mimikatz
software.description: Mimikatz is a credential dumping utility that can obtain plaintext Windows account credentials from memory, as well as perform pass-the-hash, pass-the-ticket, and Kerberos ticket attacks.

software.name: Cobalt Strike
software.description: Cobalt Strike is a commercial adversary simulation tool. It has been adopted by threat actors for post-exploitation activities including lateral movement, privilege escalation, and command and control.

software.name: PsExec
software.description: PsExec is a lightweight telnet-replacement from Sysinternals that allows execution of processes on remote systems using any user's credentials.

software.name: Net
software.description: Net is a built-in Windows utility used to manage network resources, user accounts, and services. Adversaries use it for discovery and lateral movement.

software.name: AdFind
software.description: AdFind is a free command-line Active Directory query tool used by threat actors for domain enumeration and discovery of domain trusts, permissions groups, and organizational units.

software.name: Impacket
software.description: Impacket is a collection of Python classes for working with network protocols. Threat actors use it for credential dumping, remote execution, and lateral movement.

software.name: Empire
software.description: Empire is an open-source post-exploitation framework that includes a PowerShell agent and a Python agent. It is used for a wide range of attack activities including credential harvesting and lateral movement.

software.name: Trojan.Karagany
software.description: Trojan.Karagany is a modular remote access trojan used primarily by Dragonfly/Energetic Bear and Dragonfly 2.0 in attacks against energy sector targets.

software.name: TrickBot
software.description: TrickBot is a banking trojan turned modular malware platform that has been used for credential theft, cryptocurrency mining, and as a delivery mechanism for ransomware including Ryuk and Conti.

software.name: Emotet
software.description: Emotet is a modular malware variant which is primarily used as a downloader for other malware. Emotet originally started as a banking trojan and has evolved to become a major botnet and distribution mechanism.
